title: Garlicky Silky Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A fragrant, low-sodium Moroccan chicken tagine with silky sauce, caramelized onions, and a touch of honey for subtle sweetness.

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: divided: 2 tbsp for onions, 1 tbsp for garlic and chicken

instructions: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot or tagine over medium heat. Add sliced onions and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, minced garlic, and Moroccan spice blend. Cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Stir in the chicken pieces and brown on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth, diced tomatoes, and tomato paste. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and let cook for 30-35 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add honey and lemon juice, stir, and season with black pepper (omit salt).
Continue cooking uncovered for another 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is tender and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and parsley, then remove from heat.
Serve hot over couscous or rice, garnishing with additional herbs if desired.
